svg.text: Blinky-M virtual front panel — 32-pin tap on Arduino Mega 2560
Blinky-M CPU (left) drives the Arduino Mega header (right) via the 2×18 digital header (pins 22–53). All 32 taps are INPUT — no series resistors needed. Common ground with the CPU.
→ ribbon tap — CPU drives, Mega reads
All wiring is straight-across: Mega header pin → CPU signal, one ribbon conductor each.
Grouped by bus here for clarity; the physical connector interleaves even/odd — see below.
Physical view — one 2×18 connector (even row = A bus · odd row = D bus + control)
board edge below · GND pair at the left end, 5 V pair at the right end · pins descend left → right · both rows are the same connector
’541-buffered — always driven, so plain INPUT reads clean.
Shared tri-state rail — INPUT_PULLUP for a defined idle; the CLKG-edge capture reads it settled.
Gated clock — capture strobe: firmware snapshots all 32 pins on its edge. Also an LED.
Reset line from the Clock Board (active low).
HALT flop — is the core stopped.
Flag N — branch-decision bit for FLOW.
Powers the Mega; common 5 V rail with the CPU.
Mandatory common ground with the CPU / Clock Board.

¹ No series resistors: every tap is INPUT only, so the pins draw only µA (no drop) and all signals sit in-range 0–5 V (nothing to clamp). Fit series R only on a line that could ever be Mega-driven — none here are.

² A bus is ’541-buffered and always driven → plain INPUT. The D bus is a shared tri-state rail (/RAM, /ALU, /A, /B enables); when idle it floats — INPUT_PULLUP gives a defined 0xFF, and the CLKG-edge capture reads D while it is settled.

³ Common ground with the CPU / Clock Board is mandatory; power the Mega from the header 5 V/GND. The firmware captures all 32 pins on each CLKG edge (coherent per-cycle snapshot) plus a slow heartbeat, so the panel stays live when the clock is stopped.

⁴ Firmware reports the raw 32-bit word only — signal names live in the C# host config, so re-label the panel for another board without touching the wiring. Control set is a working pick; /TRST, /ALU, ALUFN0/1, BANKEN, AMODE, /PC … drop straight into 39–53.
